seoTitle: Fix Undeposited Funds in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Clear out an inflated Undeposited Funds account in QuickBooks Online by combining pending payments into a matching bank deposit.
intro: When customer payments are recorded but never combined into a bank deposit, your Undeposited Funds account can pile up and distort your books. This guide shows how to sweep those pending payments into a proper deposit entry in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open the Bank Deposit form
steps.body: Click + New and select Bank Deposit from the Other column.

steps.heading: Choose the receiving account
steps.body: Select the checking account where the physical funds actually landed.

steps.heading: Select the pending payments
steps.body: In the 'Select the payments included in this deposit' grid, check each customer payment that matches a real-world deposit.

steps.heading: Handle processing fees
steps.body: If the total doesn't match your bank statement exactly, use the Add funds to this deposit section to record bank fees under an expense account.

steps.heading: Save and match to your bank feed
steps.body: Save and close, then check Bank Transactions to confirm the deposit matches the incoming bank feed line.

faq.question: Why is my Undeposited Funds balance so high?
faq.answer: It typically happens when customer payments are recorded but never combined into a bank deposit, leaving them stuck in the holding account.

faq.question: How do I know which payments belong together in one deposit?
faq.answer: Group payments that were physically deposited together in one bank transaction, matching the total to your bank statement.

faq.question: What if the deposit total doesn't match my bank record?
faq.answer: Use the Add funds to this deposit section to record the difference as a bank fee or other adjustment.

faq.question: How do I confirm the fix worked?
faq.answer: Run a Balance Sheet report and check that your Undeposited Funds line has dropped back down to zero.
